# SEO Article Template

> A reusable template for publishing high-quality educational content, tutorials, career guides, government schemes, and long-form articles.
>
> Replace all placeholders (`{{ }}`) with actual content before publishing.

---

# {{ Article Title }}

Example:

```
How to Prepare for SSC CGL 2026: Complete Beginner's Guide
```

---

# Meta Information

| Field | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Title | {{ SEO Title }} |
| Meta Description | {{ Meta Description }} |
| Canonical URL | {{ URL }} |
| Primary Category | {{ Category }} |
| Author | {{ Author }} |
| Published | {{ Date }} |
| Last Updated | {{ Date }} |

---

# Introduction

Briefly explain:

- What this article covers
- Why it matters
- Who should read it
- What readers will learn

Example:

```
This guide explains how beginners can prepare for SSC CGL efficiently using a structured study plan, recommended books, and previous year question papers.
```

---

# Quick Summary

| Topic | Summary |
|--------|---------|
| Target Audience | {{ Audience }} |
| Difficulty Level | {{ Beginner / Intermediate / Advanced }} |
| Reading Time | {{ Minutes }} |
| Last Updated | {{ Date }} |

---

# Table of Contents

- Introduction
- Background
- Key Concepts
- Step-by-Step Guide
- Common Mistakes
- Best Practices
- Frequently Asked Questions
- Related Resources
- Conclusion

---

# Background

Provide context before explaining the topic.

Examples:

- Why this topic is important
- Current trends
- Common misconceptions
- Eligibility (if applicable)

---

Explain important terminology.

| Term | Meaning |
|------|---------|

| {{ Term }} | {{ Explanation }} |

Avoid assuming prior knowledge.

# Best Practices

- Practice consistently.
- Use official resources where available.
- Keep information up to date.
- Verify facts before sharing.
- Follow a logical learning path.

# Practical Example

Provide a realistic example.

Example:

```
A graduate preparing for SSC CGL can begin with the official syllabus, solve previous papers, and follow a weekly study schedule to track progress.
```

# Related Resources

Link to relevant pages.

Examples:

- Related Articles
- Government Jobs
- Syllabus
- Admit Cards
- Results
- Official Websites
- Previous Papers

---

# References

List official or authoritative references.

Examples:

- Official Government Website
- Official Notification
- Official Guidelines
- Educational Resources

---

# Conclusion

Summarize:

- Main takeaways
- Key recommendations
- Next steps

Keep the conclusion concise and practical.

---

# Recommended Schema

Select the schema type that best matches the content.

| Page Type | Suggested Schema |
|------------|------------------|
| Tutorial | Article |
| Blog | Article |
| FAQ | FAQPage |
| Government Scheme | Article |
| Career Guide | Article |
| Recruitment Notice | JobPosting (where appropriate), Article |

---

# SEO Checklist

- [ ] Unique title
- [ ] Meta description
- [ ] Canonical URL
- [ ] One H1
- [ ] Logical headings
- [ ] Internal links
- [ ] External references
- [ ] Optimized images
- [ ] Structured data
- [ ] Mobile-friendly layout

---

# GEO Checklist

- [ ] Main entities identified
- [ ] Context provided
- [ ] Official references included
- [ ] Related topics linked
- [ ] Terminology consistent

---

# AEO Checklist

- [ ] Main question answered early
- [ ] FAQ section included
- [ ] Tables improve clarity
- [ ] Lists improve readability
- [ ] Answers are concise

---

# Accessibility Checklist

- [ ] Heading hierarchy
- [ ] Alt text for images
- [ ] Semantic HTML
- [ ] Readable tables
- [ ] Keyboard-friendly navigation

---

# Final Review

Before publishing:

- [ ] Proofread content
- [ ] Verify facts
- [ ] Check dates
- [ ] Test links
- [ ] Validate structured data
- [ ] Preview on mobile
- [ ] Update XML Sitemap if applicable

---

# Revision History

| Version | Date | Notes |
|----------|------|-------|
| 1.0 | {{ Date }} | Initial publication |
| 1.1 | {{ Date }} | Content update |

---

# Disclaimer

This template provides a standardized structure for educational articles.

Always verify information against official or authoritative sources before publication.
